label: Depository Institution
examples: Banks, credit unions, building societies, savings institutions
key_obligations: Report account balances and gross interest paid on accounts held by foreign tax residents.

label: Custodial Institution
description: You hold financial assets on behalf of others as a substantial part of your business.
examples: Brokers, custodians, prime brokers, nominee account providers
key_obligations: Report balances, dividends, interest, and gross proceeds from asset sales.

label: Investment Entity (Type A) - Managed by another FI
description: Your gross income is primarily from financial assets AND you are professionally managed by another financial institution.
examples: Managed funds, SPVs, unit trusts managed by a regulated fund manager
key_obligations: Treated as a Reporting FI if in a participating jurisdiction, or as a Passive NFFE if not. Report investor equity and debt interests.

label: Investment Entity (Type B) - Not managed by another FI
description: A trust or investment vehicle whose gross income is primarily from financial assets, but is NOT managed by another financial institution.
examples: Family trusts, self-managed investment vehicles, certain private equity structures
key_obligations: Treated as a Passive NFFE. Controlling persons (settlors, trustees, beneficiaries with >25% interest) must be identified and reported.

label: Specified Insurance Company
description: You issue cash value life insurance contracts or annuity contracts.
examples: Life insurers, annuity providers, bancassurance arms of banks
key_obligations: Report cash surrender value or amount paid on insurance/annuity contracts held by foreign tax residents.

label: Non-Reporting Financial Institution
description: Your institution is exempt from CRS reporting obligations under the standard.
examples: Governmental entities, international organisations, central banks, certain regulated pension funds, exempt collective investment vehicles
key_obligations: Limited or no reporting obligations. Due diligence may still be required to confirm exempt status. Consult local guidance.
